behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: This is mainly an artifact I/O contract check: run the real evaluate-stage module with a real artifact store and verify key/path written. Pure unit mocks can miss wiring/key-name regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage against a real local artifact backend in a test run context; assert `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is not written by evaluate.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Needs real module wiring plus artifact read/write behavior in one stage. No external API or LLM is required to catch the storage contract failure.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage with deterministic inputs, assert it reads grounding-influenced data path and writes output to `plan` while leaving `grounding` intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The risk is unintended side-effect writes through real stage/artifact plumbing; integration catches that better than isolated function tests.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive with real artifact storage, and assert no `plan` write/update occurs from deep-dive in this branch.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Lineage bugs are usually context ID / storage-scope wiring issues inside persistence boundaries; real local persistence is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Within one real run context, execute evaluate to write `grounding`, then execute implement and assert it resolves that exact artifact lineage (same context/run identifiers).

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly validates end-to-end multi-step sequencing and cross-step influence. Real failure often appears only across the full chain, not single-stage tests.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full chain in order within one run using stable prompts/fixtures; assert final `plan` content includes decisions traceable to prior `grounding` output and that intermediate artifacts remain correctly separated.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Fallback correctness is mostly branching plus artifact I/O safety. A real local artifact backend is enough to detect corruption/regression.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement in a context with no `grounding` artifact but with unrelated existing artifacts; assert implement succeeds, writes a valid `plan`, and leaves unrelated artifacts unchanged.
